_measure_avg_time_ms(func: callable, iterations: int = 1000) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Measure average execution time in milliseconds.\"\"\"\n    times = []\n    for _ in range(iterations):\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n        func()\n        end = time.perf_counter()\n        times.append((end - start) * 1000)  # Convert to ms\n    return sum(times) / len(times)\n\n\ndef Centralized utility for parsing LLM responses.\n\nHandles:\n1. ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = content[0].get(\"text\", \"\")\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(f\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: dict,\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","numLines":264,"startLine":1,"totalLines":264}}}

def test_malformed_json_in_json_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Malformed JSON inside ```json fence should raise clear error.\"\"\"\n        malformed = '```json\\n{invalid json here}\\n```'\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError, match=\"Failed to parse JSON\"):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def test_malformed_json_in_plain_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Malformed JSON inside ``` fence should raise clear error.\"\"\"\n        malformed = '```\\n{broken: }\\n```'\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError, match=\"Failed to parse JSON\"):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def test_unclosed_bracket_in_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Unclosed bracket in fence should fail with clear error.\"\"\"\n        malformed = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"\"\"Trailing comma in fence should fail.\"\"\"\n        malformed = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\",}\\n```'\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError, match=\"Failed to parse JSON\"):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def test_missing_quotes_in_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Missing quotes around keys in fence should fail.\"\"\"\n        malformed = '```json\\n{key:          parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Nested/Malformed Fence Patterns\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestNestedFencePatterns:\n    \"\"\"Test edge cases with backticks inside JSON content.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_backtick_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Backtick character inside JSON string value should be preserved.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"text\": \"code `example` here\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"text\": \"code `example` here\"}\n\n    def test_triple_backtick_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Triple backticks inside JSON string should be handled correctly.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"code\": \"example ``` here\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"code\": \"example ``` here\"}\n\n    def test_multiple_backticks_in_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple backtick sequences in string should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"a\": \"`foo`\", \"b\": \"```bar```\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"a\": \"`foo`\", \"b\": \"```bar```\"}\n\n    def test_fence_in_nested_object(self):\n        \"\"\"Backticks in nested object values should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"outer\": {\"inner\": \"value `test`\"}}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"outer\": {\"inner\": \"value `test`\"}}\n\n    def test_backtick_in_array(self):\n        \"\"\"Backticks in array values should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"items\": [\"a\", \"`b`\", \"c\"]}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"items\": [\"a\", \"`b`\", \"c\"]}\n\n    def test_leading_backtick_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Backtick right after opening fence should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"`value\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"`value\"}\n\n    def test_trailing_backtick_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Backtick right before closing fence should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value`\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value`\"}\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Unicode and Special Characters\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestUnicodeAndSpecialCharacters:\n    \"\"\"Test Unicode and special character handling in fenced content.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_unicode_emoji_in_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Emojis in JSON should parse correctly.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"message\": \"Hello 🌍🚀\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"message\": \"Hello 🌍🚀\"}\n\n    def test_unicode_chinese_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Chinese characters should parse correctly.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"text\": \"你好世界\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"text\": \"你好世界\"}\n\n    def test_unicode_arabic_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Arabic characters should parse correctly.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"text\": \"مرحبا بالعالم\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"text\": \"مرحبا بالعالم\"}\n\n    def test_unicode_cyrillic_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Cyrillic characters should parse correctly.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"text\": \"Привет мир\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"text\": \"Привет мир\"}\n\n    def test_newlines_in_json_strings(self):\n        \"\"\"Newlines in JSON string values should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"text\": \"line1\\\\nline2\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"text\": \"line1\\nline2\"}\n\n    def test_tabs_in_json_strings(self):\n        \"\"\"Tabs in JSON string values should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"text\": \"col1\\\\tcol2\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"text\": \"col1\\tcol2\"}\n\n    def test_special_escape_sequences(self):\n        \"\"\"Various JSON escape sequences should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"text\": \"Quote: \\\\\", backslash: \\\\\\\\, slash: /\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"text\": 'Quote: \", backslash: \\\\, slash: /'}\n\n    def test_unicode_escape_sequences(self):\n        \"\"\"Unicode escape sequences should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"text\": \"\\\\u0048\\\\u0065\\\\u006C\\\\u006C\\\\u006F\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result == {\"text\": \"Hello\"}\n\n    def test_mixed_unicode_and_literal(self):\n        \"\"\"Mixed Unicode escapes and literals should work.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"greeting\": \"Hello\", \"emoji\": \"\\\\uD83D\\\\uDE00\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"greeting\"] == \"Hello\"\n        # emoji unicode should decode to 😀\n        assert len(result[\"emoji\"]) == 2  # UTF-16 surrogate pair\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Large Payload Tests\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestLargePayloads:\n    \"\"\"Test large JSON payloads to ensure no memory issues.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_large_nested_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Large deeply nested structure should parse.\"\"\"\n        # Create a large nested structure (~50KB)\n        large_obj = {\"level\": 0}\n        current = large_obj\n        for i in range(1, 1000):\n            current[\"next\"] = {\"level\": i}\n            current = current[\"next\"]\n\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_obj)\n        fenced = f'```json\\n{large_json}\\n```'\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"level\"] == 0\n        assert result[\"next\"][\"level\"] == 1\n\n    def test_large_wide_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Large wide structure (many keys) should parse.\"\"\"\n        # Create a wide structure with many keys (~100KB)\n        large_obj = {f\"key_{i}\": f\"value_{i}\" for i in range(5000)}\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_obj)\n        fenced = f'```json\\n{large_json}\\n```'\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result) == 5000\n        assert result[\"key_0\"] == \"value_0\"\n        assert result[\"key_4999\"] == \"value_4999\"\n\n    def test_large_array_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Large array structure should parse.\"\"\"\n        # Create large array (~80KB)\n        large_array = [{\"id\": i, \"data\": f\"item_{i}\"} for i in range(10000)]\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_array)\n        fenced = f'```json\\n{large_json}\\n```'\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result) == 10000\n        assert result[0][\"id\"] == 0\n        assert result[9999][\"id\"] == 9999\n\n    def test_large_unicode_payload(self):\n        \"\"\"Large payload with Unicode content should parse.\"\"\"\n        # Mix of Unicode characters in large payload\n        large_obj = {\n            f\"section_{i}\": {\n                \"emoji\": \"🌍🚀⭐\",\n                \"chinese\": \"中文测试\",\n                \"arabic\": \"اختبار\",\n                \"cyrillic\": \"тест\",\n            }\n            for i in range(1000)\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_obj)\n        fenced = f'```json\\n{large_json}\\n```'\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result) == 1000\n        assert result[\"section_0\"][\"emoji\"] == \"🌍🚀⭐\"\n\n    def test_very_large_fence_payload(self):\n        \"\"\"Very large payload (>500KB) should parse without memory issues.\"\"\"\n        # Create a very large payload (~600KB)\n        large_obj = {\n            \"data\": [\"x\" * 100 for _ in range(6000)],  # 600KB+\n            \"metadata\": {\"size\": \"large\"}\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_obj)\n        fenced = f'```json\\n{large_json}\\n```'\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result[\"data\"]) == 6000\n        assert len(result[\"data\"][0]) == 100\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Fence Edge Cases\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestFenceEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test edge cases specific to fence handling.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_no_closing_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Missing closing fence should still parse JSON.\"\"\"\n        # Without closing fence, the parser strips opening and returns rest\n        incomplete = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        result = parse_llm_response(incomplete)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_no_opening_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Only closing fence should be treated as plain JSON.\"\"\"\n        closing_only = '{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(closing_only)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_multiple_closing_fences(self):\n        \"\"\"Multiple closing fences - first should be used.\"\"\"\n        multiple = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(multiple)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_fence_without_language(self):\n        \"\"\"Fence without language specifier should work.\"\"\"\n        no_lang = '```\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(no_lang)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_fence_with_whitespace_language(self):\n        \"\"\"Fence with whitespace language should work.\"\"\"\n        whitespace_lang = '```   \\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(whitespace_lang)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_fence_case_sensitivity(self):\n        \"\"\"Fence is case-sensitive - JSON not json should fail.\"\"\"\n        upper_case = '```JSON\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        # Case-sensitive fence should be stripped anyway (starts with ```)\n        result = parse_llm_response(upper_case)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_extra_content_after_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Content after closing fence should be ignored.\"\"\"\n        extra = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```\\n extra content here'\n        result = parse_llm_response(extra)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_content_before_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Content before opening fence should break parsing.\"\"\"\n        before = 'some text ```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError, match=\"Failed to parse JSON\"):\n            parse_llm_response(before)\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Strip Fences Behavior\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestStripFencesBehavior:\n    \"\"\"Test strip_fences parameter behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_strip_fences_false_preserves_content(self):\n        \"\"\"strip_fences=False should preserve fence markers.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced, strip_fences=False, expect_json=False)\n        assert result.strip().startswith('```json')\n        assert '```' in result\n\n    def test_strip_fences_true_removes_markers(self):\n        \"\"\"strip_fences=True (default) should remove fence markers.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced, strip_fences=True, expect_json=False)\n        assert not result.strip().startswith('```json')\n        assert '```' not in result\n\n    def test_strip_fences_false_with_valid_json(self):\n        \"\"\"strip_fences=False with valid JSON in fences should fail.\"\"\"\n        fenced = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError, match=\"Failed to parse JSON\"):\n            parse_llm_response(fenced, strip_fences=False)\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Error Message Quality\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestErrorMessageQuality:\n    \"\"\"Ensure error messages are helpful for debugging.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_error_includes_snippet(self):\n        \"\"\"ParseLLMError should include response snippet.\"\"\"\n        malformed = '```json\\n{this is broken}\\n```'\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError) as exc_info:\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n        error_msg = str(exc_info.value)\n        assert \"Response snippet:\" in error_msg\n\n    def test_error_snippet_length(self):\n        \"\"\"Error snippet should be truncated.\"\"\"\n        # Create a long malformed JSON\n        long_malformed = '```json\\n' + '{' + ', ' + 'broken' * 1000 + '}'\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError) as exc_info:\n            parse_llm_response(long_malformed)\n        error_msg = str(exc_info.value)\n        # Snippet should be truncated (check it's not the full string)\n        assert len(error_msg) < len(long_malformed)\n\n    def test_error_preserves_raw_response(self):\n        \"\"\"ParseLLMError should preserve raw response for debugging.\"\"\"\n        malformed = '```json\\n{broken}\\n```'\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError) as exc_info:\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n        assert exc_info.value.raw_response == malformed\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Real-world Edge Cases\n# test_extra_newlines_around_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Extra newlines around JSON in fence should work.\"\"\"\n        extra_newlines = '```json\\n\\n\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n\\n\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(extra_newlines)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_trailing_whitespace_in_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Trailing whitespace after JSON in fence should work.\"\"\"\n        trailing = '```json\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n   \\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(trailing)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_leading_whitespace_in_fence(self):\n        \"\"\"Leading whitespace before JSON in fence should work.\"\"\"\n        leading = '```json\\n   \\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(leading)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_mixed_line_endings(self):\n        \"\"\"Mixed line endings (\\\\r\\\\n and \\\\n) should work.\"\"\"\n        mixed = '```json\\r\\n{\"key\": \"value\"}\\n```'\n        result = parse_llm_response(mixed)\n        assert result == {\"key\": \"value\"}\n\n    def test_indentation_in_multiline_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Pretty-printed JSON with indentation should work.\"\"\"\n        pretty = '```json\\n{\\n  \"key\": \"value\",\\n  \"nested\": {\\n    \"a\": 1\\n  }\\n}\\n```'\n        result = result[\"explain\"]\n\n    def test_incomplete_fence_at_start(self):\n        \"\"\"Test string starting with only two backticks.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"``json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip anything (doesn't start with 3 backticks)\n        assert result == fenced\n\n    def test_fence_without_language_specifier(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with content but no language specifier.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_with_extra_backticks(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with 4+ backticks.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"````json\\n{content}\\n````\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # The function strips the first ``` and then rsplit removes another ````\n        # leaving one backtick - this is expected behavior for non-standard fence\n        assert result == f\"{content}\\n`\"\n\n    def test_fence_no_newline_after_opening(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with no newline after opening marker.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        # Not standard markdown but some LLMs might do this\n        fenced = f\"```json{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # When there's no newline after opening fence, the function's split logic\n        # results in empty string because it splits on first \\n which is after the content\n        assert result == \"\"\n\n    def test_multiple_closing_fences(self):\n        \"\"\"Test content with multiple closing fence markers.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\", \"note\": \"end ```\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # rsplit with maxsplit=1 handles this\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_in_middle_of_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence marker appearing within JSON structure.\"\"\"\n        # This is malformed but tests parsing behavior\n        content = '{\"key1\": \"value1\", \"```\": \"fence\", \"key2\": \"value2\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"```\"] == \"fence\"\n\n\n# \"\\\\\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"newline\"] == \"\\n\"\n        assert result[\"tab\"] == \"\\t\"\n \"items\": [\n                {\n                    \"id\": i,\n                    \"name\": f\"item-{i}\",\n                    \"description\": \"x\" * 100,  # 100 char description\n                    \"tags\": [f\"tag-{j}\" for j in range(10)],\n                    \"metadata\": {\n                        \"created\": 1234567890,\n                        \"updated\": 1234567890,\n                        \"active\": True\n                    }\n                }\n                for i in range(5000)  # ~5000 items\n            ]\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_data)\n result[\"items\"][0][\"id\"] == 0\n\n    def test_large_nested_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test deeply nested JSON structure.\"\"\"\n        # Create a deeply nested structure\n        data = {\"level\": 0}\n        current = data\n        for i in range(100):  # 100 levels deep\n            current[\"nested\"] = {\"level\": i + 1}\n            current = current[\"nested\"]\n        current[\"value\"] = \"deep\"\n\n        large_json = json.dumps(data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"level\"] == 0\n\n    def test_large_array_with_unicode(self):\n        \"\"\"Test large array with many unicode entries.\"\"\"\n        data = {\n            \"unicode_items\": [\n                f\"item-{i}-你好-😊-مرحبا\"\n                for i in range(1000)\n            ]\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n test_large_payload_performance_reasonable(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that large payload parsing completes in reasonable time.\"\"\"\n        import time\n\n        # Create a 500KB payload\n        large_data = {\"data\": [\"x\" * 1000 for _ in range(500)]}\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start\n\n        assert result is not None\n        # Should complete in less than 1 second even for large payload\n        assert elapsed < 1.0, f\"Large payload took {elapsed:.3f}s (too slow)\"\n\n    def test_malformed_large_payload_clear_error(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that malformed large payload gives clear error.\"\"\"\n        # Create a large malformed JSON\n        malformed = '{\"data\": [' + ', '.join(['\"x\"' * 100 for _ in range(1000)]) + '}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{malformed}\\n```\"\n\n whitespace patterns.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # Whitespace before opening fence\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(f\"   ```json\\n{content}\\n```\")\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Whitespace after opening fence (but before newline)\n        fenced = f\"```json   \\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n # Lowercase (standard)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Uppercase (non-standard but should still strip)\n        fenced = f\"```JSON\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Mixed case\n        fenced = f\"```Json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_with_language_dialect(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert \"key\" in result\n\n    def test_single_backtick_no_strip(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that single backtick is not treated as fence.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"`json\\n{content}\\n`\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip (only 1 backtick)\n        assert result.startswith(\"`json\")\n\n    def test_double_backtick_no_strip(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that double backtick is not treated as fence.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"``json\\n{content}\\n``\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip (only 2 backticks)\n        assert result.startswith(\"``json\")\n\n    def f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```   \"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # rsplit should handle trailing spaces\n        assert result == content\n\n    def like a fence\n        content = '{\"text\": \"This is not a fence: ```json\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should strip the ============================================================================\n\nclass TestNestedAndMalformedFencePatterns:\n    \"\"\"Test edge cases with fence patterns within content.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_triple_backtick_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON containing triple backticks as a value.\"\"\"\n        # Use escaped newlines in JSON (\\\\n becomes \\n after parsing)\n        json_with_backticks = r'{\"code\": \"```python\\nprint(\\\"hello\\\")\\n```\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{json_with_backticks}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"code\"] == \"```python\\nprint(\\\"hello\\\")\\n```\"\n\n    def test_multiple_fences_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON containing multiple fence markers.\"\"\"\n        json_with_fences = '{\"description\": \"Use ``` for code blocks\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{json_with_fences}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert '{\"explain\": \"The fence pattern ``` is used for markdown\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert \"```\" in result[\"explain\"]\n\n    def test_incomplete_fence_at_start(self):\n        \"\"\"Test string starting with only two backticks.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"``json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip anything (doesn't start with 3 backticks)\n        assert result == fenced\n\n    def test_fence_without_language_specifier(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with content but no language specifier.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_with_extra_backticks(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with 4+ backticks.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"````json\\n{content}\\n````\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # The function strips the first ``` and then rsplit removes another ````\n        # leaving one backtick - this is expected behavior for non-standard fence\n        assert result == f\"{content}\\n`\"\n\n    def test_fence_no_newline_after_opening(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with no newline after opening marker.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        # Not standard markdown but some LLMs might do this\n        fenced = f\"```json{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # When there's no newline after opening fence, the function's split logic\n        # results in empty string because it splits on first \\n which is after the content\n        assert result == \"\"\n\n    def test_multiple_closing_fences(self):\n        \"\"\"Test content with multiple closing fence markers.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\", \"note\": \"end ```\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # rsplit with maxsplit=1 handles this\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_in_middle_of_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence marker appearing within JSON structure.\"\"\"\n        # This is malformed but tests parsing behavior\n        content = '{\"key1\": \"value1\", \"```\": \"fence\", \"key2\": \"value2\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"```\"] == \"fence\"\n\n\n# TestUnicodeAndSpecialCharacters:\n    \"\"\"Test handling of Unicode and special characters in fenced content.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_unicode_emoji_in_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with emoji characters.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"status\": \"✅\", \"mood\": \"😊\", \"weather\": \"🌤️\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"status\"] == \"✅\"\n        assert result[\"mood\"] == \"😊\"\n        assert result[\"weather\"] == \"🌤️\"\n\n    def test_unicode_chinese_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with Chinese characters.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"name\": \"张三\", \"city\": \"北京\", \"message\": \"你好世界\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"name\"] == \"张三\"\n        assert result[\"city\"] == \"北京\"\n        assert result[\"message\"] == \"你好世界\"\n\n    def test_unicode_arabic_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with Arabic characters (RTL text).\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"greeting\": \"مرحبا\", \"name\": \"أحمد\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"greeting\"] == \"مرحبا\"\n        assert result[\"name\"] == \"أحمد\"\n\n    def test_unicode_cyrillic_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with Cyrillic characters.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"greeting\": \"Привет\", \"name\": \"Иван\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"greeting\"] == \"Привет\"\n        assert result[\"name\"] == \"Иван\"\n\n    def test_special_escape_sequences(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with various escape sequences.\"\"\"\n        content = r'{\"newline\": \"\\n\", \"tab\": \"\\t\", \"quote\": \"\\\"\", \"backslash\": \"\\\\\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"newline\"] == \"\\n\"\n        assert result[\"tab\"] == \"\\t\"\n        assert result[\"quote\"] == '\"'\n        assert result[\"backslash\"] == \"\\\\\"\n\n    def test_unicode_surrogate_pairs(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with characters requiring surrogate pairs.\"\"\"\n        # Using character outside BMP\n        content = '{\"symbol\": \"𝕿𝖍𝖎𝖘 𝖎𝖘 𝖆 𝖙𝖊𝖘𝖙\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert \"𝕿\" in result[\"symbol\"]\n\n    def test_mixed_line_endings(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with mixed line endings (\\\\r\\\\n vs \\\\n).\"\"\"\n        content = '{\\r\\n\"key\": \"value\"\\r\\n}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\r\\n{content}\\r\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"key\"] == \"value\"\n\n    def test_null_character_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON containing null character via escaped unicode.\"\"\"\n        # Using \\\\u0000 which is valid JSON escape for null character\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\\\\u0000end\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        # After JSON parsing, the null character should be in the string\n        assert result[\"key\"] == \"value\\x00end\"\n\n    def test_zero_width_joiner(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with emoji zero-width joiner sequences.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"family\": \"👨‍👩‍👧‍👦\", \"skin_tone\": \"👋🏽\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        # These should be preserved\n        assert len(result[\"family\"]) > 0\n        assert len(result[\"skin_tone\"]) > 0\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Large Payload Edge Cases\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestLargePayloadEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test very large payloads to ensure no memory issues.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_very_large_payload_no_crash(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that very large payload doesn't cause crashes.\"\"\"\n        # Create a JSON payload ~1MB in size\n        large_data = {\n            \"items\": [\n                {\n                    \"id\": i,\n                    \"name\": f\"item-{i}\",\n                    \"description\": \"x\" * 100,  # 100 char description\n                    \"tags\": [f\"tag-{j}\" for j in range(10)],\n                    \"metadata\": {\n                        \"created\": 1234567890,\n                        \"updated\": 1234567890,\n                        \"active\": True\n                    }\n                }\n                for i in range(5000)  # ~5000 items\n            ]\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        # Should parse without error\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result[\"items\"]) == 5000\n        assert result[\"items\"][0][\"id\"] == 0\n\n    def test_large_nested_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test deeply nested JSON structure.\"\"\"\n        # Create a deeply nested structure\n        data = {\"level\": 0}\n        current = data\n        for i in range(100):  # 100 levels deep\n            current[\"nested\"] = {\"level\": i + 1}\n            current = current[\"nested\"]\n        current[\"value\"] = \"deep\"\n\n        large_json = json.dumps(data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"level\"] == 0\n\n    def test_large_array_with_unicode(self):\n        \"\"\"Test large array with many unicode entries.\"\"\"\n        data = {\n            \"unicode_items\": [\n                f\"item-{i}-你好-😊-مرحبا\"\n                for i in range(1000)\n            ]\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result[\"unicode_items\"]) == 1000\n        assert \"你好\" in result[\"unicode_items\"][0]\n\n    def test_large_payload_performance_reasonable(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that large payload parsing completes in reasonable time.\"\"\"\n        import time\n\n        # Create a 500KB payload\n        large_data = {\"data\": [\"x\" * 1000 for _ in range(500)]}\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start\n\n        assert result is not None\n        # Should complete in less than 1 second even for large payload\n        assert elapsed < 1.0, f\"Large payload took {elapsed:.3f}s (too slow)\"\n\n    def test_malformed_large_payload_clear_error(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that malformed large payload gives clear error.\"\"\"\n        # Create a large malformed JSON\n        malformed = '{\"data\": [' + ', '.join(['\"x\"' * 100 for _ in range(1000)]) + '}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{malformed}\\n```\"\n\n whitespace patterns.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # Whitespace before opening fence\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(f\"   ```json\\n{content}\\n```\")\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Whitespace after opening fence (but before newline)\n        fenced = f\"```json   \\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert \"key\" in result\n\n    def test_fence_case_sensitivity(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that fence language specifiers are case-sensitive.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # Lowercase (standard)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Uppercase (non-standard but should still strip)\n        fenced = f\"```JSON\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Mixed case\n        fenced = f\"```Json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_with_language_dialect(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with language dialect specifier.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # With dialect\n        fenced = f\"```json/python\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert \"key\" in result\n\n    def test_single_backtick_no_strip(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that single backtick is not treated as fence.\"\"\"\n        content result.startswith(\"`json\")\n\n    def test_double_backtick_no_strip(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that double backtick is not treated as fence.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"``json\\n{content}\\n``\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip (only 2 backticks)\n        assert result.startswith(\"``json\")\n\n    def between fences.\"\"\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(\"```json\\n   \\n```\")\n        assert result == \"\"\n\n    def test_parse_llm_response_empty_string_raises(self):\n        \"\"\"Test assert result == \"\"\n\n    def test_parse_llm_response_whitespace_only_no_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Test whitespace-only string is returned when not expecting JSON.\"\"\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(\"  \\n  \", expect_json=False)\n        assert result == \"  \\n  \"\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Malformed JSON with fences.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_malformed_json_missing_closing_brace(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with missing closing brace inside fences.\"\"\"\n        malformed = \"```json\\n{\\\"key\\\": \\\"value\\\"\\n```\"\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError) as exc_info:\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n        assert \"Failed to parse JSON\" in str(exc_info.value)\n\n    def test_malformed_json_missing_opening_brace(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with missing opening brace inside fences.\"\"\"\n        malformed = \"```json\\n\\\"key\\\": \\\"value\\\"}\\n```\"\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def test_malformed_json_trailing_comma(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with trailing comma inside fences.\"\"\"\n        malformed = \"```json\\n{\\\"key\\\": \\\"value\\\",}\\n```\"\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def pytest.raises(ParseLLMError):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def test_malformed_json_invalid_escape_sequence(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with invalid escape sequence inside fences.\"\"\"\n        malformed = \"```json\\n{\\\"key\\\": \\\"value\\\\x\\\"}\\n```\"\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def pytest.raises(ParseLLMError):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def test_malformed_json_comma_instead_of_colon(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with comma instead of colon.\"\"\"\n        malformed = \"```json\\n{\\\"key\\\", \\\"value\\\"}\\n```\"\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n    def test_malformed_json_extra_comma(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with extra comma inside fences.\"\"\"\n        malformed = \"```json\\n{\\\"key\\\": \\\"value\\\",,}\\n```\"\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError):\n            parse_llm_response(malformed)\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Nested/Malformed Fence Patterns\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestNestedAndMalformedFencePatterns:\n    \"\"\"Test edge cases with fence patterns within content.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_triple_backtick_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON containing triple backticks as a value.\"\"\"\n        # Use escaped newlines in JSON (\\\\n becomes \\n after parsing)\n        json_with_backticks = r'{\"code\": \"```python\\nprint(\\\"hello\\\")\\n```\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{json_with_backticks}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"code\"] == \"```python\\nprint(\\\"hello\\\")\\n```\"\n\n    def test_multiple_fences_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON containing multiple fence markers.\"\"\"\n        json_with_fences = '{\"description\": \"Use ``` for code blocks\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{json_with_fences}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"description\"] == \"Use ``` for code blocks\"\n\n    def test_nested_fence_like_pattern(self):\n        \"\"\"Test content that looks like nested fences but isn't.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"explain\": \"The fence pattern ``` is used for markdown\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert \"```\" in result[\"explain\"]\n\n    def test_incomplete_fence_at_start(self):\n        \"\"\"Test string starting with only two backticks.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"``json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip anything (doesn't start with 3 backticks)\n        assert result == fenced\n\n    def test_fence_without_language_specifier(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with content but no language specifier.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_with_extra_backticks(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with 4+ backticks.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"````json\\n{content}\\n````\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # The function strips the first ``` and then rsplit removes another ````\n        # leaving one backtick - this is expected behavior for non-standard fence\n        assert result == f\"{content}\\n`\"\n\n    def test_fence_no_newline_after_opening(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with no newline after opening marker.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        # Not standard markdown but some LLMs might do this\n        fenced = f\"```json{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # When there's no newline after opening fence, the function's split logic\n        # results in empty string because it splits on first \\n which is after the content\n        assert result == \"\"\n\n    def test_multiple_closing_fences(self):\n        \"\"\"Test content with multiple closing fence markers.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\", \"note\": \"end ```\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # rsplit removes the LAST ``` marker, which in this case is the extra one\n        # So we get content up to (but not including) the final ```\n        assert result == f\"{content}\\n```\\n\"\n\n    def test_fence_in_middle_of_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence marker appearing within JSON structure.\"\"\"\n        # This is malformed but tests parsing behavior\n        content = '{\"key1\": \"value1\", \"```\": \"fence\", \"key2\": \"value2\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"```\"] == \"fence\"\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Unicode and Special Characters\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestUnicodeAndSpecialCharacters:\n    \"\"\"Test handling of Unicode and special characters in fenced content.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_unicode_emoji_in_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with emoji characters.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"status\": \"✅\", \"mood\": \"😊\", \"weather\": \"🌤️\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"status\"] == \"✅\"\n        assert result[\"mood\"] == \"😊\"\n        assert result[\"weather\"] == \"🌤️\"\n\n    def test_unicode_chinese_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with Chinese characters.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"name\": \"张三\", \"city\": \"北京\", \"message\": \"你好世界\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"name\"] == \"张三\"\n        assert result[\"city\"] == \"北京\"\n        assert result[\"message\"] == \"你好世界\"\n\n    def test_unicode_arabic_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with Arabic characters (RTL text).\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"greeting\": \"مرحبا\", \"name\": \"أحمد\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"greeting\"] == \"مرحبا\"\n        assert result[\"name\"] == \"أحمد\"\n\n    def test_unicode_cyrillic_characters(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with Cyrillic characters.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"greeting\": \"Привет\", \"name\": \"Иван\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"greeting\"] == \"Привет\"\n        assert result[\"name\"] == \"Иван\"\n\n    def test_special_escape_sequences(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with various escape sequences.\"\"\"\n        content = r'{\"newline\": \"\\n\", \"tab\": \"\\t\", \"quote\": \"\\\"\", \"backslash\": \"\\\\\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"newline\"] == \"\\n\"\n        assert result[\"tab\"] == \"\\t\"\n        assert result[\"quote\"] == '\"'\n        assert result[\"backslash\"] == \"\\\\\"\n\n    def test_unicode_surrogate_pairs(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with characters requiring surrogate pairs.\"\"\"\n        # Using character outside BMP\n        content = '{\"symbol\": \"𝕿𝖍𝖎𝖘 𝖎𝖘 𝖆 𝖙𝖊𝖘𝖙\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert \"𝕿\" in result[\"symbol\"]\n\n    def test_mixed_line_endings(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with mixed line endings (\\\\r\\\\n vs \\\\n).\"\"\"\n        content = '{\\r\\n\"key\": \"value\"\\r\\n}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\r\\n{content}\\r\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"key\"] == \"value\"\n\n    def test_null_character_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON containing null character via escaped unicode.\"\"\"\n        # Using \\\\u0000 which is valid JSON escape for null character\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\\\\u0000end\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        # After JSON parsing, the null character should be in the string\n        assert result[\"key\"] == \"value\\x00end\"\n\n    def test_zero_width_joiner(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with emoji zero-width joiner sequences.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"family\": \"👨‍👩‍👧‍👦\", \"skin_tone\": \"👋🏽\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        # These should be preserved\n        assert len(result[\"family\"]) > 0\n        assert len(result[\"skin_tone\"]) > 0\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Large Payload Edge Cases\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestLargePayloadEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test very large payloads to ensure no memory issues.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_very_large_payload_no_crash(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that very large payload doesn't cause crashes.\"\"\"\n        # Create a JSON payload ~1MB in size\n        large_data = {\n            \"items\": [\n                {\n                    \"id\": i,\n                    \"name\": f\"item-{i}\",\n                    \"description\": \"x\" * 100,  # 100 char description\n                    \"tags\": [f\"tag-{j}\" for j in range(10)],\n                    \"metadata\": {\n                        \"created\": 1234567890,\n                        \"updated\": 1234567890,\n                        \"active\": True\n                    }\n                }\n                for i in range(5000)  # ~5000 items\n            ]\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        # Should parse without error\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result[\"items\"]) == 5000\n        assert result[\"items\"][0][\"id\"] == 0\n\n    def test_large_nested_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test deeply nested JSON structure.\"\"\"\n        # Create a deeply nested structure\n        data = {\"level\": 0}\n        current = data\n        for i in range(100):  # 100 levels deep\n            current[\"nested\"] = {\"level\": i + 1}\n            current = current[\"nested\"]\n        current[\"value\"] = \"deep\"\n\n        large_json = json.dumps(data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"level\"] == 0\n\n    def test_large_array_with_unicode(self):\n        \"\"\"Test large array with many unicode entries.\"\"\"\n        data = {\n            \"unicode_items\": [\n                f\"item-{i}-你好-😊-مرحبا\"\n                for i in range(1000)\n            ]\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result[\"unicode_items\"]) == 1000\n        assert \"你好\" in result[\"unicode_items\"][0]\n\n    def test_large_payload_performance_reasonable(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that large payload parsing completes in reasonable time.\"\"\"\n        import time\n\n        # Create a 500KB payload\n        large_data = {\"data\": [\"x\" * 1000 for _ in range(500)]}\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start\n\n        assert result is not None\n        # Should complete in less than 1 second even for large payload\n        assert elapsed < 1.0, f\"Large payload took {elapsed:.3f}s (too slow)\"\n\n    def test_malformed_large_payload_clear_error(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that malformed large payload gives clear error.\"\"\"\n        # Create a large malformed JSON\n        malformed = '{\"data\": [' + ', '.join(['\"x\"' * 100 for _ in range(1000)]) + '}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{malformed}\\n```\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError) as exc_info:\n            parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        # Error message should include snippet\n        assert \"Failed to parse JSON\" in str(exc_info.value)\n        # Raw response should be available\n        assert exc_info.value.raw_response is not None\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Additional Fence Edge Cases\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestAdditionalFenceEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test additional edge cases around fence handling.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_fence_with_extra_whitespace_variations(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with various whitespace patterns.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # Whitespace before opening fence\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(f\"   ```json\\n{content}\\n```\")\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Whitespace after opening fence (but before newline)\n        fenced = f\"```json   \\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert \"key\" in result\n\n    def test_fence_case_sensitivity(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that fence language specifiers are case-sensitive.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # Lowercase (standard)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Uppercase (non-standard but should still strip)\n        fenced = f\"```JSON\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Mixed case\n        fenced = f\"```Json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_with_language_dialect(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with language dialect specifier.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # With dialect\n        fenced = f\"```json/python\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert \"key\" in result\n\n    def test_single_backtick_no_strip(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that single backtick is not treated as fence.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"`json\\n{content}\\n`\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip (only 1 backtick)\n        assert result.startswith(\"`json\")\n\n    def test_double_backtick_no_strip(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that double backtick is not treated as fence.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"``json\\n{content}\\n``\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip (only 2 backticks)\n        assert result.startswith(\"``json\")\n\n    def test_fence_with_trailing_spaces_on_closing(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with spaces after closing marker.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```   \"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # rsplit should handle trailing spaces\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_multiple_fences_only_first_stripped(self):\n        \"\"\"Test content with fence-like opening that isn't actually a fence.\"\"\"\n        # This tests a case where content contains what looks like a fence\n        content = '{\"text\": \"This is not a fence: ```json\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should strip the      content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should strip opening fence and return rest\n        assert
